100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On May 19, 1926, tensions escalated in Britain as a general strike entered its second week, with labor unions demanding better wages and working conditions. Meanwhile, in Germany, the government faced increasing political instability, as inflation continued to ravage the economy. Across the Atlantic, the United States experienced a surge in automotive production, signaling a strong recovery in the post-war economy.
